summary: IDF forces engaged a cross‑border infiltrator near the Lebanon border, killing the attacker and conducting area scans. Israeli strikes eliminated two senior Islamic Jihad commanders in southern Gaza. Iranian officials warned foreign forces near Iranian waters of constant danger and urged them to leave.
key_points: IDF ground and air units killed an infiltrating gunman from Lebanon in the Ramim Ridge area and closed a nearby highway (Source: @manniefabian/58525).
Israeli airstrike in southern Gaza killed Islamic Jihad commander Iyad Nofal and cell leader Ahmed Marouf (Source: @manniefabian/58523).

Givati Brigade troops operating north of the Litani River in southern Lebanon seized Hezbollah weapons caches containing firearms, anti‑tank missiles, launchers and drones (Source: @shin_persian/6782).
Iranian foreign minister warned foreign forces near Iranian waters of “constant risk” from human error or accidental exchange of fire and advised them to depart (Source: @New_security8200/201511).
A U.S. KC‑46A Pegasus refueling aircraft was tracked at 4,750 ft over Palestine (Source: @redlinkleb/23718).
A U.S. Army Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz; crew rescued, cause under investigation (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3656).
Iranian officials claimed that U.S. assets worth $3 billion were transferred from Abu Dhabi to Tehran in exchange for halting attacks on Israel (Source: @SharghDaily/164882).

sections.executive_summary: IDF forces killed an infiltrating gunman from Lebanon in the Ramim Ridge area and closed a nearby highway (Source: @manniefabian/58525). Israeli airstrike in southern Gaza eliminated two senior Islamic Jihad commanders (Source: @manniefabian/58523). Iranian foreign minister warned foreign forces near Iranian waters of constant danger and urged them to leave (Source: @New_security8200/201511).

points: Iranian foreign minister warned foreign forces operating near Iranian waters that they face constant risk from human error, simple accidents, or possible exchange of fire, and recommended they leave the area (Source: @New_security8200/201511).
Another Iranian foreign minister statement echoed the same warning, emphasizing diplomatic language but noting the ability to speak other languages if needed (Source: @New_security8200/201510).
An Iranian official reiterated that foreign forces near Iran are at permanent danger and that the best solution is their departure (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368257).
Iranian officials claimed that U.S. assets valued at $3 billion were transferred from Abu Dhabi to Tehran in exchange for stopping attacks on Israel (Source: @SharghDaily/164882).
Iranian parliament member warned that any U.S. violation of the cease‑fire would be met with decisive Iranian defense of its interests (Source: @naya_foriraq/77954).
Iranian foreign minister again urged foreign forces to keep distance to reduce risk, preferring diplomatic language (Source: @asafroz/50044).
Iranian spokesperson said no new proposal had been sent to the United States, indicating a diplomatic stalemate (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368270).

IDF reported a gunman infiltrated from Lebanon into the Ramim Ridge area, was shot dead, and the area was scanned by an Israeli Air Force drone; nearby border communities were ordered to stay indoors and a highway was closed (Source: @manniefabian/58525).
IDF spokesperson said forces opened fire on a suspected terrorist near the Lebanon border, eliminated the attacker, and continued extensive ground and air scans (Source: @idf_telegram/24255).
IDF confirmed a second similar incident with initial fire reports, no Israeli casualties, and ongoing scans by ground forces and Air Force aircraft (Source: @idf_telegram/24252).
Israeli strike in southern Gaza killed Islamic Jihad commander Iyad Nofal and cell leader Ahmed Marouf, who were planning attacks on IDF forces (Source: @manniefabian/58523).

Givati Brigade operating north of the Litani River in southern Lebanon located several Hezbollah weapons depots in Zawtar al‑Sharqiyah and Zawtar al‑Gharbiya, seizing firearms, anti‑tank missiles, launchers, drones and other gear (Source: @shin_persian/6782).

Israeli forces reportedly struck the coastal city of Tyre, issuing an evacuation warning for the entire city and its outskirts, including a previously un‑evacuated Christian neighborhood (Source: @manniefabian/58522).
IDF reported that no injuries occurred during the cross‑border engagement and that they remain in continuous contact with local authorities (Source: @idf_telegram/24255).
Two senior Islamic Jihad members, commander Iyad Nofal and cell leader Ahmed Marouf, were killed in an Israeli strike in the southern Gaza Strip (Source: @manniefabian/58523).
The IDF stated the two terrorists were planning imminent attacks against IDF forces before being eliminated (Source: @manniefabian/58523).

Givati Brigade troops operating north of the Litani River in southern Lebanon identified and seized multiple Hezbollah weapons caches in Zawtar al‑Sharqiyah and Zawtar al‑Gharbiya, capturing firearms, anti‑tank missiles, launchers, drones and other equipment (Source: @shin_persian/6782).

A gunman who infiltrated from Lebanon into Israeli territory at Ramim Ridge opened fire on Israeli troops and was shot dead; the area was subsequently scanned by an IDF drone and a nearby highway was closed (Source: @manniefabian/58525).

Israeli strikes hit the coastal city of Tyre, prompting an evacuation order for the city and its outskirts, including a Christian neighborhood not previously ordered to evacuate (Source: @manniefabian/58522).
A U.S. Army Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz; both crew members were rescued and the cause remains under investigation (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3656).
Reports indicated extensive flights of U.S. fighter aircraft over Iraq (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72986).
A U.S. KC‑46A Pegasus aerial refueling aircraft was tracked at 4,750 ft over Palestine, indicating ongoing U.S. logistical operations in the region (Source: @redlinkleb/23718).
U.S. fighter jets conducted broad operations over Iraq, suggesting heightened aerial activity in the Gulf area (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72986).

key_developments: IDF ground and air units killed an infiltrating gunman from Lebanon in the Ramim Ridge area and closed a nearby highway (Source: @manniefabian/58525).
Israeli airstrike in southern Gaza eliminated Islamic Jihad commander Iyad Nofal and cell leader Ahmed Marouf (Source: @manniefabian/58523).
Givati Brigade seized Hezbollah weapons caches north of the Litani River, capturing firearms, anti‑tank missiles, launchers and drones (Source: @shin_persian/6782).
Iranian foreign minister warned foreign forces near Iranian waters of constant risk and urged them to depart (Source: @New_security8200/201511).
U.S. KC‑46A Pegasus refueling aircraft was tracked over Palestine at 4,750 ft (Source: @redlinkleb/23718).
U.S. Army Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz; crew rescued, cause under investigation (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3656).
Iranian officials claimed $3 billion in assets were transferred from Abu Dhabi to Tehran in exchange for halting attacks on Israel (Source: @SharghDaily/164882).
sections.threat_assessment: Cross‑border infiltration from Lebanon remains a tangible threat, with IDF forces responding swiftly to neutralize attackers. Israeli strikes in Gaza continue to target high‑level Islamic Jihad operatives, indicating sustained offensive pressure. Iranian warnings to foreign forces near its waters suggest a heightened risk of accidental or intentional engagements, potentially drawing regional actors into broader confrontations.
sections.regional_response: Israel is maintaining aggressive posture along its northern border and in Gaza, employing both ground and air assets to eliminate threats. Iran is leveraging diplomatic rhetoric to pressure foreign military presence, while also hinting at possible retaliatory measures. The United States continues logistical and combat aviation operations in the region, reflecting ongoing involvement despite diplomatic tensions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of border infiltration routes is critical as attackers exploit the Lebanon‑Israel frontier. Continued surveillance of Hezbollah arms depots in southern Lebanon is essential for pre‑empting future attacks. Iranian statements about foreign force risks should be correlated with any observed movements of U.S. or allied assets near the Strait of Hormuz and surrounding waters.
